chores.title: Edit + delete "I owe {partner}" debts on the settlement page
chores.description: Partner-debt entries (Expense{paidBy:'gf'}) can be created from the settlement page but never edited, and only deleted from the expenses list. They should be editable + deletable on the settlement page, where they belong. Prereq for BUG-1's 'route to settlement to edit' flow.
chores.plan: New updatePartnerDebt action (mirror addPartnerDebt + IDOR-safe updateForUser; preserve paidBy:'gf'/cardId:null/isShared:false/yourPercentage:1/actualExpenditure=amount/no subcategory). PartnerDebtForm gains an edit mode (optional debt prop, prefill via getExpenseForEdit, 'Save changes'). New client SettlementJournalInteractive adds edit + delete (reuse deleteExpense) dialogs on partner_debt rows; router.refresh after. Out of scope: transfers/funded-card/your_expense rows, card-payment editing, all expenses-screen changes (that's BUG-1).

chores.title: Decouple partner money from cards — drop the funded-card-payment flag
chores.description: The 'card payment funded by Brenda' flag encodes two real events as one and spawned the edge cases you hit (money-in vs money-in-then-card vs kept-as-cash). Per the model, money from Brenda is just money in; a card payment is just a card payment; they're never coupled.
chores.plan: Foundation (depends on CHORE-4). (1) Remove Movement.fundedByPartner + the 'funded_card_payment' settlement journal kind. (2) Money-from-Brenda = a plain gf_received transfer-in; a card payment = a plain card_payment — two independent events. (3) settlement.service: moneyPartnerPaidYou = Σ gf_received only (drop the fundedByPartner term from the sum). (4) Simplify the settlement help / info-icon copy now that the edge cases are gone. (5) Prod data migration: existing fundedByPartner card payments → split into a gf_received (money in) + a plain card_payment (reviewed SQL on real data).

chores.title: "I owe {partner}" becomes settlement-only — not an expense (reverses ADR-0018)
chores.description: Today 'I owe Brenda' is stored as Expense{paidBy:'gf'}, so it pollutes the expenses list, categories, budget, and spend-by-card (that's BUG-1's phantom Cash). Per the agreed cash-basis model, a thing she fronted is HER spending and only a debt to you — it must live ONLY in settlement, never in your expenses/consumption. Foundation of the money-model rework.
chores.plan: Reverses ADR-0018 — write the superseding ADR + short spec first (docs/decisions is protected: get sign-off). Then: (1) store the debt as a Movement (new type 'gf_fronted' — she fronted, you owe; amount/date/note, no card) instead of an Expense; addPartnerDebt/updatePartnerDebt write the Movement. (2) settlement.service: yourDebtToPartner = Σ gf_fronted movements (was Σ actualExpenditure of paidBy:'gf'); the journal 'partner_debt' kind reads from movements; re-point CHORE-1's edit/delete onto the movement. (3) Drop paidBy:'gf' from every read — expenses list, dashboard categories/radar/buckets, getCardSpends (phantom gone for free); deprecate Expense.paidBy (all 'you'). (4) Prod data migration: convert existing paidBy:'gf' expenses → gf_fronted movements (real data — Neon MCP survey + reviewed SQL, careful). Retires BUG-1 and the whole 'debt shows as an expense' confusion.

chores.title: Edit + delete money movements (card payments + transfers), with optional note
chores.description: Card payments and settlement transfers are delete-only (delete + re-add), and transfers can't be edited at all — surfaced when a $350 payment was logged as $320 with no way to fix it. Make both editable + deletable, mirroring CHORE-1's settlement-debt edit, and give transfers an optional note. Least urgent of the rework.
chores.plan: New updateMovement action (IDOR-safe, scoped by userId). Edit mode on the movement forms (card-payment form + transfer form); optional free-text note on transfers ('what it was toward' — a label, never a budget category). Reuse CHORE-1's dialog pattern in the settlement journal + the expenses feed. Depends on CHORE-3 (final transfer shape after the funded-card flag is gone).

chores.title: Settings foundation — configurable partner behind an opt-in sharing toggle
chores.description: Make the app usable by anyone: partner identity becomes per-user data behind an opt-in 'I share expenses' toggle, instead of the hardcoded PARTNER_NAME='Brenda' constant (used in 13 files). Foundation of CHORE-6 (spec 0006) — the Settings page and the shared/solo split both build on this flag. The Settings page is currently an empty stub.
chores.plan: Kicks off with an ADR (partner-as-Settings + opt-in sharing mode; docs/decisions is protected — sign-off first). (1) Schema: Settings +sharesExpenses(Boolean, default false) +partnerName(String?); defaultSharePercentage already exists. (2) A getSettings(userId) accessor as the single source; remove the PARTNER_NAME constant and thread the name down as data (no component imports a partner constant). (3) Settings page shell (server component reads Settings) + the Expense-split-rule block: an 'I share expenses' toggle that reveals + requires partner name and your share % when on. Persist via an IDOR-safe server action (name required when toggle on). (4) Owner-account migration: sharesExpenses=true, partnerName='Brenda' (no record rewrites). BUG-2 rider: the same manual owner prod-migration trip also re-seeds prod Category.color (gray→per-slug) and FK-safely deletes the retired Amex Gold card — documented in docs/operations/owner-shared-mode-migration.md, no app code. Tests: action validation, accessor, form both modes. Out of scope: the gating sweep (6.b), card management (6.c), and the deferred mock sections (Account/Preferences/Privacy/Export/theme).

chores.title: Solo mode — hide the shared-expense + settlement machinery when sharing is off
chores.description: With the sharesExpenses flag in place (CHORE-6.a), a solo user must see a plain expense tracker: no split control, no settlement, no partner anywhere. Today that machinery is always on. Depends on CHORE-6.a.
chores.plan: When sharesExpenses=false, hide/guard: (1) the shared/% control in components/expense/ExpenseForm.tsx (expenses save isShared:false, yourPercentage:1, actualExpenditure=amount); (2) the Settlement route + its link in components/nav/AppNav.tsx, plus a server-side route guard (solo user hitting the URL → redirect); (3) the partner items in the Add menu (components/expense/AddExpenseButton.tsx → gf_received/gf_paid/gf_fronted actions); (4) partner widgets on the dashboard. Shared mode renders exactly as today. Tests: each surface in both modes; route guard. Depends on CHORE-6.a.

chores.title: Card management — add / rename / recolor / archive cards in Settings
chores.description: Cards are seeded only; there is no way to add, rename, recolor, or remove one from the app. Add card management to the Settings page. Delete is unsafe (cards are referenced by Expense[] and Movement[]), so removal is soft-archive. Depends on CHORE-6.a (the Settings page shell).
chores.plan: Full card vertical slice (none exists today). (1) Schema: Card +archivedAt(DateTime?). (2) lib/palette.ts: shared named palette (seeded colours + extras) — ends the seed↔login-page hex drift; seed + login import it. (3) lib/schemas/card.ts: Zod (name 1–40, type credit|debit|cash, color validated #RRGGBB — swatch OR custom hex). (4) lib/repositories/card.repository.ts: listActive (archivedAt:null, pickers), listForSettings (all), create, updateForUser, archiveForUser, deleteForUser (IDOR-safe where:{id,userId}; delete uses deleteMany + re-checks Expense/Movement refs, refuses if any). (5) app/_actions/card/{add,update,archive,delete}.ts mirroring save-split-rule (Zod→auth→repo→ActionResult; codes validation|unauthenticated|has_references|not_found|limit_reached|duplicate_name|db_error). (6) Settings Cards section (components/settings/CardsForm.tsx) replaces the coming-soon chip: list + Add form + per-card edit (rename/recolor swatch+hex) + Archive(used)/Delete(unused). (7) Picker reads (expenses/page, dashboard/page) filter archivedAt:null; history reads unfiltered. Rules: Cash fully locked (no edit/archive/delete); archive used / hard-delete unused; unique active name per user (case-insensitive); cap 10 active (MAX_ACTIVE_CARDS); no unarchive. Deferred to CHORE-9: outstanding-balance warn/block on archive. Tests: CRUD actions, validation, IDOR scoping, delete-refuses-when-referenced, hex validation, cap + duplicate-name, archive hides-from-picker-keeps-history, palette module, CardsForm both states. Depends on CHORE-6.a. Full design: docs/specs/0006-settings-standalone-shared-modes.md §6.

chores.title: Toggle dashboard categories between my-share and total spend
chores.description: CANCELLED (2026-07-22): shared-only feature — the toggle is inert in solo mode (a solo user's share IS the total), and the expected prod user base is solo, so the value-to-effort is low. Revisit only if a shared-mode user actually asks for it. Original intent: The dashboard Categories cards + 'Where the money went' show only your share (actualExpenditure, the 68% slice). Add a control to flip them to the full charged amount (ignoring the split) — e.g. Transport shows $2,000 total, not just your part. Aligns with CHORE-6 / spec 0006: in solo mode a user's share IS the total, so the category displays must already support a total basis; build this so the two dovetail (a shared user toggles; a solo user is effectively always 'total').
chores.plan: Scope TBD at build time (decide with the user): a segmented 'My share ⇄ Total' control on the dashboard. Thread both sums per category from the dashboard data (amount-sum + actualExpenditure-sum) so the toggle is a pure client re-scope of the Categories cards + 'Where the money went'. Open question: whether the 50/25/25 buckets + 'Income vs spent' also re-scope (they encode budget-against-your-income, so likely stay my-share). Coordinate with CHORE-6.a/.b (sharesExpenses flag) so solo mode reuses the total-basis path rather than duplicating it.

chores.title: Per-user categories, subcategories, and budgets (multi-tenancy foundation)
chores.description: Category, Subcategory, and CategoryBudget are global (no userId) — one shared config for the whole DB. With public signup (8.d) every user would share the same category set, colors, and monthly budgets; one user editing a budget changes it for everyone. Expenses ARE userId-scoped, so spend stays private, but the scaffolding leaks. Make categories/subcategories/budgets per-user before any signup. Reverses ADR-0016 (budgets deliberately global for the single-user app). Foundation for CHORE-8.b–8.e.
chores.plan: Kicks off with a superseding ADR (reverses ADR-0016; docs/decisions is protected — sign-off first). (1) Schema: +userId on Category, Subcategory, CategoryBudget (relations to User); change CategoryBudget @@unique([categoryId,month]) -> @@unique([userId,categoryId,month]). (2) Owner data migration: backfill userId = owner on all existing Category/Subcategory/CategoryBudget rows so nothing orphans; dev via prisma migrate, PROD via a documented reviewed-SQL runbook (mirror spec 0005's careful-real-data approach — Neon MCP survey before/after). (3) Scope every category/budget read + write by userId: dashboard.repository (getCategorySpends/getCategoryBreakdown), category.repository, category-budget.repository, the set-budget action, seed. (4) Tests: per-user isolation (user A's budget/color invisible to B), userId scoping on every read/write, migration correctness. Depends on nothing new; blocks 8.b/8.c/8.d/8.e.

chores.title: New-user starter kit — default categories/subcategories/cards/settings
chores.description: A single provisioning routine that seeds a fresh account's starting data so a new user lands in a working, private tracker. Shared by the owner seed AND signup (8.d) so the two never drift. Depends on CHORE-8.a (per-user categories).
chores.plan: (1) A provisionNewUser(userId) domain/service routine that seeds, all userId-scoped: the default 13 categories + subcategories (the frozen reference in domain-reference.md), a default Cash card (REQUIRED — Cash is un-addable via the UI since 6.c) plus optional generic Credit + Debit starters, and a default Solo Settings row (sharesExpenses=false). (2) Refactor prisma/seed.ts to provision the owner via the same routine (DRY). (3) Tests: a freshly provisioned user has the full default set, isolated from other users; seed still works. Depends on 8.a.

chores.title: Per-user category management in Settings
chores.description: Now that categories are per-user (8.a), give users a Settings 'Categories' section to manage them — mirroring the Cards section from 6.c. Rename / recolor / add / set monthly budget / archive. Makes the seeded default set adjustable per account.
chores.plan: Mirror the CHORE-6.c card vertical slice for Category: Zod schema, IDOR-safe userId-scoped CRUD actions, a Settings Categories section (client form), reads. Decide exact ops at pickup (rename/recolor/add/set-budget/archive; delete only if unused, else archive — categories are referenced by Expense[]). Coordinates with the existing inline dashboard budget editing (set-budget) so they share the same per-user path. Depends on 8.a.

chores.title: Public signup, capped to a configurable max users in PROD
chores.description: Add a real signup page, bounded: PROD accepts at most N users (default 10) so onboarding stays small and controlled. Reverses the 'no signup' deferral in spec 0006 §8. A new account is provisioned via the 8.b starter kit so it lands in a clean, private Solo tracker. Depends on 8.a + 8.b.
chores.plan: (1) Signup page (app/(auth)/signup, mirror the login page/form -> server action pattern) + public server action: email + password + display name (Zod; password strength), bcrypt.hash(pw,10) like the seed, create User then provisionNewUser (8.b). Replace the SignUpComingSoon placeholder on the login page with a real link. (2) User cap: MAX_USERS env var (default 10) checked BEFORE create — db.user.count vs cap, reject at-cap with a clear 'signups are currently full' message; document in .env.example + coding-conventions.md. (3) After signup, sign the user in (auto) and route to onboarding (8.e). (4) Empty-state: dashboard shows a 'set your income' nudge when a user has no income (not a dead 0/0/0). (5) Update spec 0006 §8. Tests: happy path, duplicate-email reject, at-cap boundary (N-1 ok, N rejected), validation, password hashed (never plain). Out of scope: email verification, password reset, OAuth, rate-limiting beyond the cap. Depends on 8.a + 8.b.

chores.title: Signup onboarding wizard
chores.description: After a user signs up (8.d), walk them through a short multi-step onboarding (modal/wizard) to set up their account instead of dropping them on an empty dashboard. Built against a user-provided Claude design reference.
chores.plan: A multi-step wizard shown on first sign-in: welcome -> display name + fixed monthly income -> pick/confirm a card (from the starter kit) -> 'do you share expenses?' toggle (+ partner name + your % when on) -> budget rule (show the default 50/25/25 as a starting point) -> done. Persists via the existing settings / income / card paths (no new money model). Steps + visuals finalized against the shared design. Depends on 8.d (a just-signed-up user) + 8.a/8.b. Out of scope: re-running the wizard later (Settings already covers edits).

chores.title: Per-card outstanding balance
chores.description: Show the current outstanding balance per card — the stat deferred since ADR-0018's era, refreshed for the cash-basis money model. Read-model + display only, no new capture (expenses already carry cardId; card payments are Movement{type:'card_payment', cardId}). Converted from the old backlog slice 2.13 (PR #46, closed) because it's feedback-driven and interlocks with card management (CHORE-6.c). Logging-completeness-bound: accurate only if every charge is logged — label it 'based on logged expenses', not the bank statement.
chores.plan: (1) Read-model, userId-scoped, behind the repository (no db in components): outstanding(card) = Sum of expense.amount charged to that card MINUS Sum of movement.amount where type='card_payment' and cardId matches. Use expense.amount (the FULL charge to the card) — NOT actualExpenditure: the card was charged the full amount regardless of any expense split; card balance is source-agnostic (spec 0005 §3). (2) Surface it on the dashboard 'Spend by card' area or a dedicated per-card view (exact surface: brainstorm on pickup); label 'based on logged expenses'. (3) CHORE-6.c interaction (careful): an archived card can still carry a nonzero outstanding balance, so this display MUST still show archived cards whose balance != 0 (never hide a card you still owe on) — pickers stay archived-filtered, this display does not. Coordinate with 6.c so archiving a card with a nonzero outstanding warns/blocks. Depends on CHORE-6.c. Tests: outstanding math (charges - payments; uses amount, ignores split), userId scoping, archived-but-nonzero card still shown, zero-balance card. Out of scope: reconciling against a real bank statement, editing card balances directly.
